summary: sensivity ships a FiveM game-cheat with covert Windows persistence, process masquerade, and obfuscated remote-license beacon
details: On load, launcher.js installs an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run registry value named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points wscript.exe at a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which silently re-launches `node.exe launcher.js` hidden on every Windows logon. The same script sets `process.title='Runtime Broker'` (a real Windows system process name), spawns detached supervisors with `windowsHide:true`, writes hidden dot-files for state, and monkey-patches console.log/warn/error to rewrite 'sens.node' to 'program module' and scrub install paths from output — deliberate anti-forensics. launcher.js then runs `eval(fs.readFileSync('server.obf.js'))` against an obfuscator.io-style RC4 string-array bundle (610-entry array, ~1973 transforms) to defeat static review. The decoded server starts an Express+Socket.IO control plane that loads a 6.8 MB native addon `sens.node` whose strings ('Teleport','LeftFoot','LeftHand','Head','__licenseAccepted') identify it as a FiveM / GTA-V cheat (ESP/aimbot/teleport), and on shutdown runs PowerShell `Get-Process | Stop-Process` against `FiveM*`. The decoded server also computes a SHA-256 over HKLM MachineGuid + hostname + C: volume serial and POSTs that fingerprint plus a license key to a hardcoded `https://licenseopi.com/check.php` endpoint unrelated to any package/publisher metadata. The package description ('Sensivity Control Panel') and the OneDrive/Runtime-Broker naming actively conceal this purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
